verdict.description: The critic's judgment ONLY, never the orchestrator's outcome. `not_run` is orchestrator-only and never emitted here.

blocking_findings.description: A reader would be misled about a fact. Only the seven blocking kinds belong here (spec/06 blocking findings); anything else the orchestrator demotes to advisory. A dropped_story MUST carry a well-formed `source` receipt or it is downgraded.

advisory_findings.description: True but weaker than it should be — weak_read_through above all. Published, never gates, never enters a retry payload.

kind.description: The rubric kind (spec/06 v2 tables). The seven blocking kinds first, then the twelve advisories. The enum is a boundary guard, not the classifier: which ARRAY a kind rides in is what gates the run, and the orchestrator re-sorts by kind regardless of where the critic put it.

where.description: A path into the v2 issue, e.g. competitors.asset_her3_dxd, indications.squamous-nsclc.arena.setting_rivals.asset_ivonescimab, house_view.threat_financing.daiichi, headline.

source.description: REQUIRED on a dropped_story — the receipt. Strict mode: the key is always present; null on every other kind.

title: ResearchSwarm critic verdict (issue.json v2.0.0)
description: The one JSON object Codex must emit for a v2 per-program issue. Passed to `codex exec --output-schema` to pin the shape at the model boundary; the orchestrator re-validates it anyway (a broken critic must never silently pass), so this is a guardrail, not the guarantee. Additive alongside critic-output-schema.json (v1), which is unchanged.
